commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Let our men (that is, all Christians) also learn to excel in good and charitable works, by furnishing to others, for necessary uses, according to their wants. (Witham)

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: That is, either those that love Paul himself, or those men that are faithful.

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: And let our own also learn to maintain good works for urgent needs, that they not be unfruitful. These were not of the number to whom Churches had been entrusted, but of the number of his companions. But Apollos was the more vehement, being an eloquent man, and mighty in the Scriptures. Acts 18:24 But if Zenas was a lawyer, you say, he ought not to have been supported by others. But by a lawyer here is meant one versed in the laws of the Jews. And he seems to say, supply their wants abundantly, that nothing may be lacking to them.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
